title: Module bundlers
body:
## Definition

**Module bundlers** resolve [ES module](concept:es-modules) graphs, transform assets, and emit optimized files for browsers — [Webpack](concept:webpack), [Vite](concept:vite), Rollup, esbuild.

## Why it exists

Browsers need bundled/split output; bundlers apply [tree shaking](concept:tree-shaking), [code splitting](concept:code-splitting), and loaders.

## How it works

Entry → dependency graph → plugins/loaders (Babel, CSS) → chunks + asset hashes for cache busting.

## Common confusion

Dev server (Vite) vs production bundle (Rollup/esbuild) — different pipelines, same mental model.

## In practice

Choose Vite for modern DX; Webpack where legacy plugin ecosystem required.

## Definition

**[Module bundlers](concept:module-bundlers)** resolve [ES module](concept:es-modules) graphs, transform assets, and emit optimized files for browsers — [Webpack](concept:webpack), [Vite](concept:vite), Rollup, esbuild.

## Why it exists

Browsers need [bundled](concept:module-bundlers)/split output; [bundlers](concept:module-bundlers) [apply](concept:call-apply-bind) [tree shaking](concept:tree-shaking), [code splitting](concept:code-splitting), and loaders.

## How it works

Entry → dependency graph → plugins/loaders ([Babel](concept:babel), CSS) → chunks + asset hashes for cache busting.

## Common confusion

Dev server ([Vite](concept:vite)) vs production bundle (Rollup/esbuild) — different pipelines, same mental model.

## In practice

Choose [Vite](concept:vite) for modern DX; [Webpack](concept:webpack) where legacy plugin ecosystem required.
